name: AMI NAGATA
outreach_message: Ami — when an institution hands Zodia custody of its assets, the settlement rails underneath that relationship carry the same weight as the custody promise itself. A failure in the infrastructure layer isn't a software bug; it's a permanent, irrecoverable event for a client whose trust was institutional-grade. At FP Block, we built the Kolme framework so custodians can run dedicated application chains they fully control — not shared infrastructure where another team's upgrade window is their risk. Would you have 20 minutes for a conversation at EthCC in Cannes this June?

name: SYLVAIN PRIGENT
outreach_message: Sylvain — SG-Forge has done something genuinely difficult: issuing regulated security tokens on public infrastructure under full banking supervision. The legal exposure of that model sits precisely at the seam between the regulatory perimeter you control and the settlement substrate you don't. A minting error or forced upgrade on shared infrastructure isn't a rollback; it's a securities incident. FP Block's Kolme framework gives issuers like SG-Forge a dedicated chain with full sovereignty over execution — interoperable with public networks but not subject to their governance. Worth 20 minutes at EthCC Cannes in June to compare notes?

name: RYAN SAUGE
outreach_message: Ryan — Taurus serves banks, and banks have a single tolerance for infrastructure failure when holding client assets: zero. The question that keeps growing as tokenization scales is not whether your custody logic is sound, but whether the rails your custody logic runs on are ones you actually control. FP Block built the Kolme framework so institutions can operate a dedicated application chain — same interoperability with Ethereum and the broader ecosystem, none of the shared-infrastructure governance risk. If you'll be in Cannes for EthCC this June, we'd value 20 minutes with you.

name: JAKE RO
outreach_message: Jake — at the scale BitGo operates, the infrastructure underneath the custody logic is itself a systemic risk variable. Hundreds of billions in assets under custody means a forced network upgrade, a sequencer decision, or a shared-chain congestion event isn't an inconvenience — it's a potential settlement failure with cascading consequences. FP Block built Kolme so custodians can own the entire execution environment, not just the keys. We'd welcome 20 minutes with you at EthCC in Cannes this June to talk through what application-specific infrastructure looks like at institutional scale.

name: SEBASTIAN WIDMANN
outreach_message: Sebastian — Komainu sits at an unusual intersection: the institutional credibility of Nomura, the hardware expertise of Ledger, and the market access of CoinShares, all wrapped in regulated custody. The one variable that doesn't fully sit inside that consortium is the execution environment where client assets settle. Compliance and insurance cover a lot, but neither undoes a permanent on-chain event caused by infrastructure you don't govern. FP Block's Kolme framework lets custodians run sovereign application chains with full control over the settlement substrate while staying interoperable with Ethereum and Cosmos. Would you have 20 minutes at EthCC in Cannes this June?

name: PATRICK HANSEN
outreach_message: Patrick — USDC's position as regulated financial infrastructure means every policy and compliance decision Circle makes is only as durable as the execution layer it lives on. The regulatory clarity you've worked to establish doesn't extend to the shared infrastructure where USDC actually settles — and when that layer makes decisions, Circle inherits the consequences. FP Block's Kolme framework lets stablecoin issuers operate on dedicated chains they fully govern, preserving all the interoperability that drives USDC's utility without ceding control of the rails. Would 20 minutes at EthCC in Cannes this June be useful?

name: STERLING BARNETT
outreach_message: Sterling — the systemic importance of USDC creates an unusual problem: Circle's compliance posture is rigorous, but the moment USDC settles on infrastructure Circle doesn't govern, the legal permanence of any error belongs to the issuer, not the network. At the volumes USDC moves, the cost of a single irreversible infrastructure-level event isn't contained by the compliance framework around it. FP Block built Kolme so issuers can run the execution environment under their own governance — interoperable with Ethereum and the broader ecosystem, but sovereign where it counts. Would you have 20 minutes at EthCC Cannes in June to dig into this?

name: JULIAN GRIGO
outreach_message: Julian — Safe's multi-signature architecture is one of the most battle-tested account models in the ecosystem, and the assets it secures reflect that trust directly. The part of the risk surface that a multi-sig model doesn't fully address is the execution environment the account lives on — when the substrate beneath a Safe instance behaves unexpectedly, the permanence of the outcome falls entirely on the locked assets. FP Block's Kolme framework is built for teams operating at this level of criticality: a dedicated application chain with full execution sovereignty, interoperable with Ethereum but not beholden to its shared governance. Happy to walk through the architecture with you for 20 minutes at EthCC in Cannes this June.

name: ANYA
outreach_message: Anya — GK8's air-gapped cold vault model is built on the premise that the most dangerous attack surface is the one connected to the outside. The corollary that's harder to solve is what happens when the execution layer your vault interacts with during settlement decisions is one you don't control — not an external attack, but an infrastructure-level event that's just as permanent. FP Block's Kolme framework lets institutions own the full settlement substrate, not just the signing layer. We'd welcome 20 minutes with you at EthCC in Cannes this June to talk through how application-specific chains complement a defense-in-depth custody model.

FP Block's Kolme framework gives issuers a dedicated chain with full governance over settlement execution while remaining interoperable with Ethereum and the broader ecosystem. name: JEAN-MARC STENGER

What's worth examining as that model scales is the governance gap between the regulatory perimeter Societe Generale controls and the public execution layer where those tokens actually live. name: SHEALTIELANZ

name: KIRK BAIRD
outreach_message: Kirk — Sigma Prime's audit work covers a critical gap: the contracts protocols deploy are only as safe as the assumptions their auditors are permitted to make about the execution environment. FP Block's Kolme framework is designed to give protocol teams infrastructure they fully own, so the substrate assumptions in an audit are actually verifiable, not inherited. Worth 20 minutes at EthCC in Cannes this June to compare how we think about that boundary?

FP Block's Kolme framework is built for exactly this layer: giving issuers and infrastructure operators sovereign execution environments that rating agencies can evaluate with the same rigor as traditional counterparty risk.
